input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when theme changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime by clearing and merging dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Android restarts the Activity on config changes.**
By default, Android destroys and recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In your `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode  // ← prevents restart
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Then detect the system change and apply your theme:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    var mergedDictionaries = Resources.MergedDictionaries;
    mergedDictionaries.Clear();
    mergedDictionaries.Add(e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark
        ? new DarkTheme()
        : new LightTheme());
};
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` everywhere you want live updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the Activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app regarding dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from their ResourceDictionary themes. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the Android system dark mode changes, the Activity is destroyed and recreated by default. They need to handle the configuration change themselves by adding `ConfigChanges` to their Activity attribute.

Let me give a concise answer.
